concerns: US-001 test at line 192 checks for `id="app"` in index.html — adding child divs inside `#app` is safe, but the plan should explicitly note that `<div id="app">` must remain as-is (it does, just worth calling out)

The plan's test approach of parsing CSS files as strings is brittle — CSS property ordering, whitespace, and shorthand variations (e.g., `inset: 0` vs `top:0;right:0;bottom:0;left:0`) can cause false negatives. Consider testing against the actual computed styles via happy-dom or simply asserting file contents with flexible regex

Step 4 says 'replace the placeholder textContent assignment' in main.ts but the new logic is just querying containers that already exist in the static HTML — the plan should clarify what replaces the placeholder text (nothing meaningful needs to run, so the main.ts change is essentially just removing the textContent line and adding CSS imports)

pointer-events:none on #overlay-ui is correctly flagged as a downstream concern — ensure the plan or a comment in shell.css notes that interactive children need pointer-events:auto, since US-003 itself won't add any overlay children to validate this
